title: Sunday, June 10
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: June 10, 2018
Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Second Sunday after Pentecost in the church calendar, falling within Ordinary Time. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would be 1 Samuel 15:34-16:13 (David's anointing), Psalm 20, 2 Corinthians 5:6-10 (Paul on faith and judgment), and Mark 4:26-34 (parables of the kingdom—the growing seed and mustard seed). This is early in the post-Pentecost season, still in the long stretch of green Ordinary Time extending toward Advent.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the interim period between General Conventions (the 79th Convention had concluded in Austin in June 2018, making this sermon date potentially during or immediately after that gathering). There were ongoing denominational discussions about immigration, refugee policy, and racial justice. The Colorado Diocese was experiencing steady pastoral leadership under Bishop Robert Wright, with no major episcopal elections or controversies specifically dominating news at this moment.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in a moderate growth phase with continued development pressures in Capitol Hill and downtown. The week of June 10 fell in the early summer season with no major weather events dominating local news. Colorado politics were focused on midterm election season preparations, with Democratic and Republican primary campaigns underway for statewide offices. The city was preparing for summer tourism season and outdoor recreation activities.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The dominant national story was the Trump administration's immigration policy, specifically the separation of migrant children from their parents at the southern border—the "family separation" crisis was reaching peak media coverage in early June 2018. The Supreme Court had recently ruled in favor of banning partisan gerrymandering in Maryland (June 4). The NBA Finals between the Golden State Warriors and Cleveland Cavaliers were underway (Game 2 was June 3), with the Warriors winning their second consecutive championship on June 8. The Supreme Court also decided Masterpiece Cakeshop v. Colorado Civil Rights Commission (June 4), a religious liberty case affecting LGBTQ+ rights—highly relevant to an Episcopal congregation. Political divisions over immigration and religious freedom were at heightened temperature.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The FIFA World Cup in Russia was beginning (opening match June 14, so heavy promotional coverage and anticipation in this week). North Korea's denuclearization remained a focal point following the Trump-Kim summit scheduled for June 12 in Singapore (just two days after this sermon). The Israeli-Palestinian conflict was simmering with ongoing tension in Gaza.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The broader cultural moment in June 2018 was one of acute political polarization in the United States, with deep divisions over immigration, religious liberty, and judicial interpretation. The #MeToo movement remained culturally prominent. There was a sense of generational anxiety about climate change and environmental policy. For Denver specifically, the city embodied both progressive politics (the Democratic-leaning Front Range) and Western individualism, creating natural tensions around government intervention on issues like immigration. The general mood was one of cultural disagreement and uncertainty about the nation's direction, with particular urgency around humanitarian issues at the southern border that would have resonated with a mainline Protestant cathedral congregation.
